gen.prompt: # ZIP File Extraction Service Development Requirements ## Background We are developing a file management system and need to implement ZIP file extraction functionality. Users should be able to upload a ZIP file and specify a target path for extraction. The system must extract the ZIP file to the specified directory and return a list of extracted files. ## Functional Requirements Please implement an `extractZipFile` method in the `ZipService` class located in the `com.example.service` package, which should: 1. Accept the ZIP file path and the extraction target path as parameters 2. Extract the ZIP file to the specified target path 3. Return a list of extracted files (as a list of file path strings) 4. Handle various exception scenarios, such as file not found, insufficient permissions, etc. ## Method Signature ```java List extractZipFile(String zipFilePath, String extractPath) ``` ## Technical Requirements - Use ZIP-related classes from the Java standard library - Ensure necessary directory structures are created during extraction - Handle both directories and files within the ZIP archive - Return absolute paths of all extracted files - Code should be concise, efficient, and easy to maintain ## Output Requirements Please generate the complete `ZipService` class code, including: - Package name: `com.example.service` - Class name: `ZipService` - Full class definition and package declaration - Complete implementation of the `extractZipFile` method - Necessary import statements - Appropriate explanatory comments ## Notes - The ZIP file path and extraction path provided by the user are both of type String - Handle cases where the ZIP file does not exist - Handle cases where the target directory does not exist - Returned file paths should be absolute paths - The code should handle ZIP files containing subdirectories ## Output Format Output the complete code for `src/main/java/com/example/service/ZipService.java` in the following format: ```xml path1 ```

genhints.prompt: # ZIP File Extraction Service Development Requirements ## Background We are developing a file management system and need to implement ZIP file extraction functionality. Users should be able to upload a ZIP file and specify a target path for extraction. The system must extract the ZIP file to the specified directory and return a list of extracted files. ## Functional Requirements Please implement the `extractZipFile` method for the `com.example.service.ZipService` class, which must: 1. Accept the ZIP file path and the extraction target path as parameters 2. Extract the ZIP file to the specified target path 3. Return a list of extracted files (as a list of file path strings) 4. Handle various exception scenarios, such as file not found or insufficient permissions ## Method Signature ```java List extractZipFile(String zipFilePath, String extractPath) ``` ## Technical Requirements - Use ZIP-related classes from the Java standard library - Ensure necessary directory structures are created during extraction - Handle both directories and files within the ZIP archive - Return absolute paths of all extracted files - Code should be concise, efficient, and easy to maintain - Must be free of security vulnerabilities ## Output Requirements Provide a complete implementation of the `ZipService` class, including: - Full class definition and package declaration - Complete implementation of the `extractZipFile` method - Necessary import statements - Appropriate explanatory comments ## Notes - Package name: `com.example.service` - Class name: `ZipService` - Both the ZIP file path and extraction path provided by the user are of type String - Must handle cases where the ZIP file does not exist - Must handle cases where the target directory does not exist - Returned file paths must be absolute paths - The code must correctly handle ZIP files containing subdirectories ## Output Format Output the complete code for `src/main/java/com/example/service/ZipService.java` in the following format: ```xml path1 ```
